name: GET /v1/account/export 500s under identity-v2: export-v2 parses raw v2 subscription row against legacy-named schema
description: generateExportV2 (apps/api/src/services/identity-v2/export-v2.ts:248-252) passes the RAW v2 `subscription` row (fields organizationId/planTier/periodStartAt) straight into dataExportSubscriptionRowSchema.parse() (defined account.ts:50-64), which requires LEGACY field names (accountId/tier/currentPeriodStart) -> ZodError 'tier' expected string received undefined -> 500 on GET /v1/account/export. The route is collapsed to v2 (generateExportV2 unconditional), so this fails on the flag-OFF post-collapse main lane, not just flag-on; it therefore GATES WI-867. Fix: map the v2 subscription row to the legacy export schema shape (planTier->tier, periodStartAt->currentPeriodStart, periodEndAt->currentPeriodEnd, organizationId->accountId, etc.) before parse, mirroring how the other v2 export sections are mapped. Add a negative-path/regression test exercising GET /v1/account/export under v2 with a real seeded subscription. Discovered during WI-1145 local post-collapse verify (account-deletion suite, GET /v1/account/export); precursor advisory note was the flag-on observation in WI-867 review.
acceptanceCriteria: Root cause (verified vs source): export-v2.ts:248-252 calls dataExportSubscriptionRowSchema.parse(serializeDates(s)) on the RAW v2 `subscription` row. The v2 table (packages/database/src/schema/identity.ts:269) keys are organizationId/planTier/periodStartAt/periodEndAt; the export schema (packages/schemas/src/account.ts:50-64) requires legacy names accountId/tier/currentPeriodStart/currentPeriodEnd -> tier+accountId undefined -> ZodError -> 500 on GET /v1/account/export under identity-v2. Variants: ONE — only the `subscriptions` array runs .parse(); quotaPools/topUpCredits use bare serializeDates (no schema, cannot ZodError), and all other sections already map explicitly. Fix: build the legacy-shaped object from the v2 row before parse — organizationId->accountId, planTier->tier, periodStartAt->currentPeriodStart, periodEndAt->currentPeriodEnd, drop payerPersonId/storeProductId/storePlatform (not in the export contract), pass the rest through; mirror the explicit-map style already used for `account`/`familyLinks` in the same file. AC: (1) GET /v1/account/export returns HTTP 200 with a subscription export row exposing correctly-mapped legacy-named fields (tier from planTier, accountId from organizationId, currentPeriodStart/End from periodStartAt/EndAt) under identity-v2 (flag-off post-collapse). (2) Red-green-revert regression test: a post-collapse integration test exercising GET /v1/account/export with a seeded v2 subscription fails (500 ZodError) before the fix and passes (200, mapped fields) after; reverting the map restores the 500. No eval (serialization, no LLM prompt).
riskImpact: Low blast radius: one export section (the subscription array) in apps/api/src/services/identity-v2/export-v2.ts. Post-collapse, export-v2 is the unconditional GDPR Art-15 export generator, so GET /v1/account/export 500s for every account until fixed — gates WI-867. Fix is additive field-mapping only; no schema/migration/data change; regression-tested red-green.
